criteria: The agent has read Section 3 (Preliminary) completely to understand the SMILES sequence, molecular graph G_mol, and SMILES graph G_smi formalism used throughout the paper.
The agent has read Section 4.1 completely to understand the local attention head, global attention head, and edge update module in the encoder.
The agent has read Section 4.2 completely to understand atom and bond reaction center identifiers and the naive versus search RC detection strategies.
The agent has read Section 4.3 completely to understand the sparse local cross-attention in the decoder conditioned on the detected reaction center S_rc.
The agent has read Section 4.4 and 4.6 to understand the SMILES attention guidance loss and the full multi-task training objective.
The agent has read Appendix 7 (Algorithm 1) to understand the SMILES graph construction procedure using RDKit atom tagging.
The agent has read Appendix 8 (Algorithm 2) to understand the ground truth SMILES token alignment computation using atom mapping numbers.
The agent has read Appendix 9 (Algorithm 3 and Equation 8) to understand the multi-stage reaction center subgraph search and the reactive score formula.
The agent has read Table 1 to check the top-k accuracy results for all three Retroformer variants in both reaction class known and unknown settings.
The agent has read Table 4 to understand the six ablation configurations and which components they isolate.
The plan includes data preparation for USPTO-50K: canonicalizing SMILES with atom mapping following the GraphRetro protocol and using RDChiral to extract ground truth reaction centers.
The plan includes the SMILES graph construction (Algorithm 1) that expands the molecular graph G_mol into the SMILES graph G_smi to align atom tokens with SMILES tokens.
The plan includes the local attention head restricted to the one-hop neighborhood of each token, incorporating bond features via element-wise multiplication with the key vector.
The plan includes the edge update module that updates bond representations at each encoder layer using the concatenation of updated token features.
The plan includes the Atom RC Identifier and Bond RC Identifier as two separate FFN modules producing per-atom and per-bond reactive probabilities.
The plan includes both the naive RC detection strategy (threshold-based, used at training and inference) and the search RC strategy (subgraph search, used only at inference).
The plan includes the local-global decoder with sparse local cross-attention restricted to detected reaction center tokens S_rc and global cross-attention over the full encoder output.
The plan includes the SMILES token alignment computation (Algorithm 2) and the attention guidance loss enforced at the first global head of the last decoder layer (Guided_last).
The plan includes on-the-fly data augmentation: 50% probability of product SMILES permutation and 50% probability of reactants order permutation per training iteration.
The plan includes the full multi-task loss: L = L_LM + L_RC_bond + L_RC_atom + L_AG.
The plan specifies the model hyperparameters: 8 encoder layers, 8 decoder layers, embedding dimension 256, 8 total attention heads (4 global + 4 local), Adam optimizer with lr=1e-4, dropout=0.3.
The plan includes all three Retroformer variants: Retroformer_base (no augmentation, naive RC), Retroformer_aug (augmentation + naive RC), and Retroformer_aug+ (augmentation + search RC).
The plan includes evaluation using top-k accuracy, top-k SMILES validity (via RDKit), and top-k round-trip accuracy (via pretrained Molecule Transformer as oracle).

The code for SMILES graph construction (Algorithm 1) has been implemented: assigning RDKit atom tags, writing tagged SMILES without canonicalization, and retrieving token-level edge connections and bond features.
The bond features from Table 5 have been implemented as a feature vector: bond type (4-dim one-hot), aromatic (1), conjugated (1), and part of ring (1), for a total size of 7.

The local attention head has been implemented as Equation 1: attention restricted to one-hop neighbors N(i), with element-wise multiplication of the key vector by the bond feature matrix A_ij before the dot product.
The global attention head has been implemented as standard scaled dot-product self-attention over the full SMILES sequence.
The encoder token representation update has been implemented as Equation 2: h^{l+1} = Linear([x_global^{l+1}; x_local^{l+1}]), concatenating global and local head outputs along the hidden dimension.
The edge update module has been implemented as Equation 3: A_ij^{l+1} = A_ij^l + FFN([h_i^{l+1}; h_j^{l+1}]), using a residual connection with a fully connected layer on concatenated token features.
The Atom RC Identifier has been implemented as Equation 4: P_rc(s_i) = sigma(FFN_atom(h_i)) applied only to atom tokens s_i in V_m.
The Bond RC Identifier has been implemented as Equation 5: P_rc(e_ij) = sigma(FFN_bond(A_ij)) applied only to bonds e_ij in E_m.
The naive RC strategy has been implemented: a token is marked as reactive if P_rc(e) > 0.5 for at least one of its incident bonds AND P_rc(s) > 0.5, with special tokens guaranteed non-reactive.

The local cross-attention in the decoder has been implemented as Equation 6: y_i^{l+1}_local = sum over s_j in S_rc of sigma(q_i k_j^T / sqrt(d)) v_j, restricting attention to detected reaction center tokens only.
The decoder representation update has been implemented as Equation 7: g^{l+1} = Linear([y_global^{l+1}; y_local^{l+1}]), concatenating global and local cross-attention outputs.

The SMILES token alignment computation has been implemented as Algorithm 2: iterating over reactant atom tokens, matching by atom mapping number to product tokens, and extending the alignment via substring matching for non-atom tokens.

The attention guidance loss L_AG has been implemented as a label smoothing cross-entropy loss between the ground truth SMILES token alignment and the cross-attention weights of the first global head of the last decoder layer.
The language modeling loss L_LM has been implemented as a cross-entropy loss over the decoder's token predictions for the reactant SMILES sequence.
The reactive probability losses L_RC_atom and L_RC_bond have been implemented as binary cross-entropy losses using ground truth reaction center labels extracted by RDChiral.
The combined multi-task loss has been implemented as L = L_LM + L_RC_bond + L_RC_atom + L_AG with all four components summed without additional weighting coefficients.
On-the-fly data augmentation has been implemented: at each training iteration, with 50% probability permute the product SMILES, and with 50% probability permute the order of reactants.

The reaction center subgraph search (Algorithm 3) has been implemented: filtering atoms/bonds by alpha_atom and alpha_bond thresholds, retrieving connected components, and performing recursive pruning with maxRootSize, minLeafSize, and maxBranch constraints.

The subgraph reactive score (Equation 8) has been implemented: normalized log-sum of atom and bond reactive probabilities weighted by a Gaussian size prior phi(|V|, mu, sigma^2) with mu=5.55 and sigma=1.2.

The subgraph search hyperparameters have been set correctly: n=3, temperature T=10, beta=0.5, maxRootSize=25, maxBranch=5, and percentile thresholds k_s=40, k_e=40 for reaction class unknown and k_s=40, k_e=55 for reaction class known.
The search inference strategy has been implemented: generating k/n reactants per reaction center candidate, then ranking all k results by the sum of reaction center score and generative score.
The diversity-enforcing step in the subgraph search has been implemented: removing all candidate subgraphs (excluding the top-1) that share at least two common nodes with the top-1 subgraph.
The model architecture has been implemented with 8 encoder layers and 8 decoder layers built on the vanilla Transformer architecture.
The embedding dimension has been set to 256 and the total number of attention heads to 8, with 4 heads designated as global and 4 as local in both encoder and decoder.
The optimizer has been implemented as Adam with a fixed learning rate of 1e-4 and a dropout rate of 0.3 applied throughout the model.
The data preprocessing pipeline has been implemented: canonicalizing molecule SMILES with atom mapping using the GraphRetro protocol and using RDChiral to extract ground truth reaction center labels.
The top-k accuracy evaluation has been implemented as exact match of the full predicted reactant SMILES against the ground truth across k=1, 3, 5, 10.
The top-k SMILES validity evaluation has been implemented as Valid(k) = (1/(N*k)) * sum over N*k candidates of 1(SMILES is valid according to RDKit).

The top-k round-trip accuracy evaluation has been implemented as RoundTrip(k) = (1/(N*k)) * sum over N*k candidates of 1(Reach Ground Truth Product), using a pretrained Molecule Transformer as the forward reaction oracle.

The ablation configurations have been implemented: (a) no guidance, (b) Guided_all, (c) Guided_last, (d) without local-global encoder, (e) without local-global decoder, (f) with reaction center search.
Retroformer_base (no data augmentation, naive RC detection) has been trained successfully on 1 NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPU for 24 hours without errors.
Retroformer_aug (with on-the-fly data augmentation and naive RC detection) has been trained successfully without errors.
Retroformer_aug+ (with data augmentation and search RC strategy at inference) has been run successfully for inference and evaluation without errors.
All six ablation configurations (a)-(f) from Table 4 have been executed successfully without errors on the reaction class unknown setting.
The SMILES validity and round-trip accuracy evaluations have been executed successfully on the test set for Retroformer_aug and Retroformer_aug+.
The execution outcomes for Retroformer_base match Table 1 for reaction class unknown: top-1 accuracy of 47.9% and top-10 accuracy of 70.7%.
The execution outcomes for Retroformer_aug match Table 1 for reaction class known: top-1 accuracy of 64.0% and top-10 accuracy of 88.3%.
The execution outcomes for Retroformer_aug+ match Table 1 for reaction class unknown: top-1 accuracy of 53.2% and top-10 accuracy of 82.1%.
The execution outcomes confirm that Retroformer_aug+ achieves new state-of-the-art accuracy for template-free retrosynthesis in both reaction class known and unknown settings.

The execution outcomes for Retroformer_aug match Table 2 for top-k SMILES validity in the reaction class unknown setting: top-10 validity of 92.6%, improving over the vanilla Transformer by approximately 23.6% at top-10.
The execution outcomes for Retroformer_aug+ match Table 2 for top-k SMILES validity: top-10 validity of 96.7%, showing superior validity compared to all template-free baselines.
The execution outcomes for Retroformer_aug match Table 3 for top-k round-trip accuracy: top-10 round-trip accuracy of 57.6%, improving over the vanilla Transformer by approximately 22.0% at top-10.
The ablation study outcomes match Table 4: configuration (c) (Guided_last + local-global encoder + local-global decoder) achieves 47.9% top-1 accuracy in the reaction class unknown setting.
The ablation study outcomes match Table 4: removing the local-global encoder (d vs c) causes a 3.8% drop in top-1 accuracy (44.1% vs 47.9%), confirming the encoder's contribution.
The ablation study outcomes match Table 4: the search strategy (f vs c) boosts top-10 accuracy from 70.7% to 78.8%, a 4.9% improvement from adding reaction center search at inference.

The ablation study outcomes confirm that Guided_last (c) achieves comparable or better performance than Guided_all (b), validating the choice of enforcing alignment loss only at the last decoder layer.
